_Comment_ASPNETCORE_FORWARDEDHEADERS: Set to 'true' for Docker/container deployments to enable X-Forwarded-Host and X-Forwarded-Prefix headers (required when behind a reverse proxy like Azure App Service)

Authentication._Comment: OIDC discovery via Authority URL. Examples: 'https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/v2.0' (Entra), 'https://your-org.okta.com' (Okta), 'https://localhost:7058' (local)

BlobStorage._Comment: Unified blob storage configuration. Provider: 'Local' for filesystem, 'Azure' for Azure Blob Storage. Environment variable overrides: BlobStorage__Provider, BlobStorage__UseManagedIdentity, etc.

DurableTask._Comment: DurableTask orchestration backend: 'SqlServer' (default, uses Tenant 0 settings), 'FileSystem' for simple dev, 'AzureStorage' for cloud. SqlServer uses the same database as FHIR resources, eliminating additional infrastructure dependencies.

SqlServer._Comment: SQL Server backend for orchestration state. Uses Tenant 0 (system partition) connection string, which inherits from the tenant specified by InheritConnectionStringFromTenant (default: Tenant 1). Schema is automatically created on startup via CreateIfNotExistsAsync.

AzureStorage._Comment: Azure Storage backend for orchestration state. Supports both connection string and Managed Identity authentication.

Authorization._Comment: FHIR authorization configuration. See rbac-authorization-with-capability-enforcement.md for details.

SmartOnFhir._Comment: SMART on FHIR configuration for authorization workflows and .well-known/smart-configuration endpoint
SmartOnFhir._Comment_OAuth: OAuth 2.0 endpoint URLs for SMART on FHIR. These will be advertised in the CapabilityStatement oauth-uris extension. Configure these to point to your authorization server (e.g., Azure Entra ID, Okta, or custom OpenIddict server).

Admin._Comment: Full access to all resources and interactions

Mcp._Comment: MCP-specific role for AI/automation access to FHIR operations via Model Context Protocol

Contributor._Comment: Contributor role with full FHIR and MCP access

Clinician._Comment: Clinical staff with access to patient care resources

Tenants._Comment_Mode: Controls multi-tenancy behavior. 'Isolated' = multiple customers with separate data stores (SaaS). 'Distributed' = single customer with horizontal sharding (NOT YET IMPLEMENTED - Phase 20.2+)
Tenants._Comment_Configurations: Array of tenant configurations. TenantId 0 is RESERVED for system operations (transaction IDs). Regular tenants start at TenantId 1+

Configurations._Comment: System Partition - Reserved for transaction IDs. Shares database with Tenant 1.

Packages._Comment: Isolated mode: Each tenant can independently auto-load packages to their own database

TransactionWatcher._Comment: Automatically commits stalled transactions. Scans all active tenants periodically.

Import._Comment: FHIR bulk import concurrency and performance tuning. Controls thread pool usage to prevent request timeouts during imports.
Import._Comment_MaxConcurrentFiles: Maximum number of input files to process concurrently (default: 2). Prevents thread pool starvation by limiting nested Task.Run() creation from StreamingImportFileActivity. Each activity spawns 1 producer + ConsumerCount worker threads.
Import._Comment_ConsumerCount: Number of consumer threads per file (default: 8). Each consumer reads from the channel and writes batches to the database. Total threads per activity = 1 producer + ConsumerCount consumers.
Import._Comment_BatchSize: Resources per batch write (default: 100). Consumers accumulate this many resources before calling BatchWriteAsync. Larger batches = better throughput, smaller batches = lower latency.
Import._Comment_ChannelCapacity: Channel capacity between producer and consumers (default: 1000). Provides backpressure: producer waits if channel is full. Prevents memory exhaustion from unbounded resource queues.

Mcp._Comment: Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration for AI-accessible FHIR operations. Set Enabled to false to disable MCP endpoints and services.

Sidecar._Comment: Sidecar provider configuration for audit, authorization, metrics, and logging. Set Enabled to true to use external gRPC services instead of local implementations. Container deployments: Override via environment variables (Sidecar__Enabled, Sidecar__AuditServiceUrl, etc.)
Sidecar._Comment_Logging: Sidecar logging configuration. MinimumLogLevel controls which logs are forwarded (Trace, Debug, Information, Warning, Error, Critical). LogBatchSize and LogFlushIntervalMs control batching behavior.

SearchParameters._Comment: SearchParameter conflict resolution when multiple IGs define parameters with the same code

ConflictResolution._Comment_PackagePriorityOrder: Explicit priority list (highest priority first). When multiple IGs define the same code, the IG with highest priority wins. Example: ['hl7.fhir.us.core', 'hl7.fhir.au.base']. If null/empty, falls back to semantic versioning.
ConflictResolution._Comment_UseSemanticVersioning: Use semantic version comparison when no explicit priority configured (default: true). Newer versions win.
ConflictResolution._Comment_LogConflicts: Log warnings when conflicts are detected and resolved (default: true). Important for debugging.
ConflictResolution._Comment_EagerLoadPackageSearchParameter: Eagerly load all package search parameters at startup (default: true). When true, all SearchParameters are loaded during initialization for predictable performance. When false, parameters are loaded lazily per resource type (faster startup, slower first search).
ConflictResolution._Comment_FailStartupOnEagerLoadError: Fail startup if eager loading fails (default: false). When true, startup fails if package SearchParameters cannot be loaded. When false, logs error and continues with base parameters only.

Terminology._Comment: Terminology import settings for CodeSystem/ValueSet/ConceptMap resources
